name: Canning Spinach and Other Greens
recipeInstructions:
1. For a 7-quart canner load, plan on using approximately 28 pounds of greens.
2. For an 9-pint canner load, plan on using approximately 18 pounds of greens.
3. A bushel of greens weighs 18 pounds and yields about 3 to 9 quarts, which is roughly 4 pounds per quart.
1. Use only freshly harvested greens. Discard any wilted, discolored, diseased, or insect-damaged leaves. The leaves should be tender and attractive in color.
2. Wash greens in small batches. Drain the water thoroughly and rinse until the water runs clear.
3. Remove tough stems and ribs from the leaves.
4. Place 1 pound of greens at a time into a cheesecloth bag or blancher basket and steam for 3 to 5 minutes, or until well wilted.
5. If desired, add ½ teaspoon of salt per quart jar.
6. Fill jars loosely with greens and add fresh boiling water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
7. Seal the jars and process according to the guidelines in Table 1 and Table 2.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for Spinach and Other Greens in a dial-gauge pressure canner (hot pack).
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Processing Time: 70 minutes for pints, 90 minutes for quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb.
2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb.
4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb.
6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb.
Table 2: Recommended processing time for Spinach and Other Greens in a weighted-gauge pressure canner (hot pack).
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Processing Time: 70 minutes for pints, 90 minutes for quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb.
Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
